strengths: Interleaved thinking+tools rendering: AVA correctly reconstructs the think→tool→think→tool→response sequence, a capability absent in most competitors. Both live (LiveStreamingBlock) and historical (InterleavedThinkingSegments) paths are handled.

Reactive architecture is genuinely SolidJS-idiomatic: createMemo for derived state, fine-grained Show/For composition, accessor props threaded properly. No unnecessary createEffect chains recomputing what a memo could handle.

Streaming content uses a dual-path design: live content comes from agent signals (no store updates during streaming), completed messages come from the store. This prevents the N^2 re-render problem that plagues naive implementations.

Tool call card system is rich: ToolCallCard, CommandOutputRow (terminal-style), DiffRow (with per-line add/delete stats), and ContextGroupHeader all provide specialized views per tool type rather than a generic fallback.
MarkdownContent throttles streaming re-renders to 150ms, preventing DOM thrashing on fast token streams, while still rendering immediately on first content.
content-visibility: auto on [data-message-id] elements gives free off-screen rendering suspension — a meaningful win for long sessions without virtualisation code.

ErrorRow is contextually rich: distinguishes cancelled vs errored, shows a live countdown for rate-limit retryAfter, and surfaces actionable hints (/model, /compact, /connect) matched to the specific error type.
ThinkingRow auto-expands during streaming and auto-collapses when complete — matches the expectation of the user seeing progress in real-time, then not being distracted after.
ApprovalDock has proper keyboard shortcuts (Enter=approve, Escape=deny), aria-dialog labelling, and risk-adaptive auto-expand for high/critical operations.

Design token system is thorough: primitives → semantic → component layers, dark/light/midnight/charcoal themes, per-accent presets, syntax token overrides, reduced-motion support, and high-contrast mode all handled in a single CSS file.
MessageActions uses group-hover visibility with focus-within fallback, ensuring keyboard users can access actions without hovering.
The three-tier mid-stream messaging model (steer/follow-up/post-complete) is architecturally unique and surfaced cleanly in the UI with Alt+Enter and Ctrl+Alt+Enter bindings.
useMessageScroll uses ResizeObserver (not polling) for auto-scroll, with a correct 300px hysteresis to detect user scroll-up intent during streaming.
CommandOutputRow correctly starts its setInterval unconditionally and clears it via onCleanup — no leaked timers.
DiffRow's computeDiffStats uses a bag-of-words multiset diff, which is fast and handles repeated lines correctly without running an actual diff algorithm on large files.
model-specific reasoning effort levels are provider-aware (PROVIDER_EFFORTS map) and the PlanActSlider uses a proper animated sliding highlight rather than toggling classes.
Empty state provides 4 categorised suggestion prompts, keyboard hint bar, and a project-avatar logo — meaningful first-run guidance.

gaps.description: MessageList has a windowed-load approach (hiddenMessageCount + load-older button) but renders ALL visible messages to the DOM simultaneously without a true virtual list. In sessions with 200+ messages each containing expanded tool cards, DOM node count climbs into the thousands. content-visibility:auto helps but does not reduce the total node count.
gaps.recommendation: Adopt @tanstack/solid-virtual or a custom virtual list with fixed-height row estimation. The existing useMessageData abstraction already has the index+id maps needed. Estimated win: 60-80% DOM node reduction for long sessions.

gaps.description: InputTextArea sets max-height: 200px as an inline style but the auto-resize logic lives in use-input-state.ts (autoResize callback). There is no smooth height transition — the textarea jumps in height on input. Competitors (Claude.ai, ChatGPT) use a CSS transition on height or a shadow-div approach.
gaps.recommendation: Add transition: height 100ms ease on the textarea and use scrollHeight shadow measurement to avoid layout jank on first keypress. Cap at 200px with a visible scrollbar only above cap.

gaps.description: When the LiveStreamingBlock finalises and is replaced by the historical MessageBubble, there is a visible flash: the streaming div unmounts and the bubble mounts in its place. animate-message-in is applied but is only 150ms with translateY(8px) — noticeable jarring position shift.
gaps.recommendation: Keep the streaming block mounted during the transition and cross-fade to the settled bubble. Alternatively use a FLIP animation: capture the streaming block's position before unmount and animate the new bubble from that position.

gaps.description: MarkdownContent sets containerRef.innerHTML = renderedHtml() directly and attaches imperative event listeners (attachCopyHandlers, attachExpandHandlers) via queueMicrotask. This pattern bypasses SolidJS reactivity entirely, is hard to test, and can leave stale listeners if the component remounts while a microtask is queued. It also precludes using SolidJS portals or context inside markdown-rendered content.
gaps.recommendation: Replace with a solid-marked / solid-dompurify combo that produces a SolidJS signal-backed component tree, or at minimum move the handler attachment to a createEffect keyed on renderedHtml() with proper cleanup. The current approach has a subtle bug: if the component unmounts before the queueMicrotask fires, containerRef is undefined but attachCopyHandlers still runs.

gaps.description:
MarkdownContent renders user messages as plain

. If a user pastes a markdown table, numbered list, or code block, it renders as raw text. All major competitors render user messages with at minimum code fence syntax highlighting.
gaps.recommendation: Apply a lightweight subset of markdown rendering (code blocks, bold, inline code) to user messages. Guard with a heuristic — only parse if the content contains ``` or starts with # to avoid false positives on prose.

gaps.description: ThinkingRow renders thinking content as raw whitespace-pre-wrap text at 0.6 opacity. Anthropic's own Claude.ai and cursor render thinking blocks with markdown formatting (headers, bullet points), which makes long reasoning chains readable. AVA's current display is unreadable for complex multi-step reasoning.
gaps.recommendation: Apply renderMarkdown() to the thinking content in the expanded view. The collapsed/preview state can remain raw text since it is just a hint.

gaps.description: When the agent starts but no token/tool events have arrived yet, LiveStreamingBlock shows a typing indicator ('ava is thinking...'). However there is no indication of WHICH turn the agent is on, how many tools have been called total, or an estimated progress. Competitors (Cursor, Continue) show 'Turn 3 of 10' or a step counter.
gaps.recommendation: Expose the current turn count from useRustAgent (already tracked in useAgent.currentTurn) and show it in the LiveStreamingBlock header: 'Turn 2 • 4 tools used'.

gaps.area: No animated height collapse/expand for tool cards
gaps.description: Tool card expansion (ToolCallCard, CommandOutputRow, DiffRow) uses Show conditionally, which causes instant mount/unmount with only animate-tool-card-in on mount. There is no collapse animation — content disappears instantly when the chevron is clicked.
gaps.recommendation: Use CSS grid-template-rows: 0fr → 1fr transition (the 'grid trick') for animated collapse/expand without needing to know content height. No JS height measurement needed.

gaps.description: MessageActions is positioned absolute -top-3 right-0 inside a relative group div. The TimestampLine renders below the bubble. On assistant messages the toolbar floats above the timestamp line and can clip outside the message row padding area when the message is at the top of the viewport. There is no overflow:visible guard on the parent.
gaps.recommendation: Change the positioning to appear below the message content (like Claude.ai) rather than above it, eliminating the clip risk. Or add overflow: visible to the parent chain and ensure the toolbar is inside the chat container's padding zone.

gaps.description: ApprovalDock captures global keydown for Enter/Escape but does not trap tab focus to the dock's buttons when it is visible. A keyboard-only user could tab past the dock and trigger unrelated buttons while an approval is pending.
gaps.recommendation: Add a focus trap (e.g. focus-trap-js or a custom loop) when request is non-null. Restore focus to the previously focused element when the dock clears.

gaps.area: Empty state suggestion cards lack keyboard accessibility
gaps.description: MessageListEmpty suggestion cards handle onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave for hover styles via imperative style mutation instead of CSS :hover or class toggling. They have no onFocus/onBlur equivalents, so keyboard users see no visual hover feedback. Also these are <button> elements but have no aria-label beyond their visible text, which is fine, but they use window.dispatchEvent for communication, a coupling smell.
gaps.recommendation: Replace onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave with CSS hover styles (hover: Tailwind utilities or a CSS variable). Add onFocus/onBlur for equivalent keyboard feedback. Consider passing an onSuggestionSelect callback prop instead of coupling to a window event.

gaps.area: Timestamp and metadata overflow on small widths
gaps.description: TimestampLine in MessageBubble packs timestamp, model name, token count, cost, elapsed time, and mode into a single line at 10px font. On narrow panels or with a long model name (e.g. 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20251022') this truncates silently — the text simply overflows under the MessageActions toolbar.
gaps.recommendation: Truncate the model name to ~20 chars in formatModelName (already strips date suffix and provider prefix — consider also truncating at a max character count). Or stack the metadata into two lines on narrow widths using flex-wrap.

gaps.area: No optimistic rendering for sent messages
gaps.description: User messages are added to the session store synchronously in useAgent.run() before the IPC call. However if the IPC call fails (e.g. Tauri unavailable), the user message is left in the store with no error indicator and no rollback. The ErrorRow appears on the next assistant message slot, not on the user message that caused the failure.
gaps.recommendation: Tag optimistically-added user messages with a pending status. If the backend call fails within the run() catch block, either remove the user message or mark it with an error annotation that renders inline.

gaps.description: CommandOutputRow starts its timer with a top-level setInterval (not inside createEffect or onMount), meaning the interval starts even when the component renders for a completed tool call where isRunning() is already false. The cleanup runs correctly via onCleanup, but the timer fires at least once before cleanup.
gaps.recommendation: Move the setInterval creation inside a createEffect(() => { if (!isRunning()) return; ... }) block matching the pattern used in ToolCallCard and ToolCallRow, which handle this correctly.

gaps.description: User-attached images in ImagesBlock (MessageBubble) render as max-w-[200px] max-h-[200px] thumbnails with no click-to-enlarge. For screenshots or diagrams this is inadequate.
gaps.recommendation: Add a click handler that opens a Dialog/overlay with the full-size image. Use SolidJS Portal to render outside the message container to avoid clipping.

gaps.description: ChatView's SearchBar sets searchMatchIds and currentSearchId via handleSearchHighlight, but these are local to MessageList. When a search match is in the hidden (not-yet-loaded) older messages, the scroll-to-message call will fail silently because the DOM node doesn't exist yet. There is no automatic load-older trigger on search.
gaps.recommendation: When scrollToMessage fails to find the DOM element, call loadOlderMessages and retry with a one-time MutationObserver or after a requestAnimationFrame delay.

gaps.description: The mid-stream message queue is a great unique feature but MessageQueueBar offers only remove-by-index. Users cannot reorder queued messages, which matters when they queue multiple follow-ups in the wrong order.
gaps.recommendation: Add drag-to-reorder with the HTML5 drag API or @solid-dnd. The queue array in the store is already index-based, so a splice swap is straightforward.

polish_items.issue: The preview hint 'Ctrl+O to see full thinking' is hardcoded text but Ctrl+O is not a defined AVA keyboard shortcut anywhere in the shortcut map. This will confuse users.
polish_items.fix: Change to 'Click to expand' or wire up Ctrl+O as an actual shortcut in shortcut-defaults.ts that calls setExpanded(true) on the focused thinking row.

polish_items.issue: duration() uses Date.now() at memo creation time for completed messages (when props.isStreaming is false), so it shows '0.0s' or a stale value after the memo is first evaluated rather than the actual wall-clock thinking duration.
polish_items.fix: For completed messages, compute duration from thinkingDuration prop (if provided) only. The current startTime signal captures component mount time, not thinking start time. Pass thinking start/end timestamps from the message metadata.

polish_items.issue: The fallback (non-interleaved) layout joins all thinking segments with ''.join('') which collapses multiple distinct thinking blocks into one block, losing the visual separation between reasoning rounds.
polish_items.fix: Show each segment's thinking content as a separate ThinkingRow in the fallback path, matching the interleaved path's visual structure.

polish_items.issue: debugLog('thinking', ...) is called unconditionally inside the Show when accessor on every render, including in production builds where debugLog is presumably a no-op but the ternary (() => { const t = ...; if (t) debugLog(...); return t })() IIFE still executes.
polish_items.fix: Move the debugLog call into a createEffect or remove it. The IIFE pattern for side effects inside a JSX accessor is fragile and creates a hidden reactive dependency.

polish_items.issue: The streaming throttle timer is reset with 'if (streamRenderTimer !== null) return' which means if content changes WHILE a render is scheduled, the new content is dropped until the next change triggers a new timer. This can cause the final streamed content to not render if no token arrives after the last 150ms window.
polish_items.fix: Store the latest content at scheduling time, not at execution time: schedule the timer once, then at execution always read the current props.content.

polish_items.issue: All three components duplicate the isRunning logic, elapsed timer setup, and duration formatting. The only differences are UI chrome (terminal header vs generic header vs diff header).
polish_items.fix: Extract a useToolCallTimer(toolCall) hook that returns { isRunning, elapsed, duration } and share it across all three components.

polish_items.issue: The actions toolbar is positioned left-0 for assistant messages but the message content starts at 0 and the toolbar is also at 0, making them overlap. The group-hover opacity transition works, but the position is wrong for user messages (align='right') where MessageActions always anchors to left-0 regardless of bubble alignment.
polish_items.fix: Conditionally set right-0 vs left-0 based on message role. The isUser prop is available in the parent but not threaded to MessageActions.

polish_items.issue: computeDiffStats uses a bag-of-words multiset diff which counts unique line occurrences, not positional changes. A file where a line was moved (same content, different position) counts 0 additions and 0 deletions, even though the diff viewer shows a change. This makes the +N/-N counters misleading.
polish_items.fix: Use a proper LCS-based line diff (e.g. diff-match-patch line mode) for stats, matching what DiffViewer already uses internally.

polish_items.issue: Suggestion card hover effects are applied via onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave with imperative e.currentTarget.style mutations. This pattern is fragile in SolidJS (bypasses reactivity) and breaks on touch devices.
polish_items.fix: Use Tailwind hover: utilities directly on the button class string. The current dynamic style approach exists because the background values reference CSS variables, but CSS variables work in Tailwind arbitrary value syntax: hover:bg-[var(--surface-raised)].

polish_items.issue: Both components call props.calls.some(...) and props.calls.every(...) inside non-memoized accessor functions that re-run on every render. For groups with many calls this is wasted computation.
polish_items.fix: Wrap anyRunning, anyError, allDone in createMemo() to cache the values between renders.

polish_items.issue: The mega-group header (>2 tool calls) always uses 'bash' as the icon name when anyRunning() is true, regardless of what tools are actually running. If the agent is running a 'read' tool inside a large group, a terminal icon appears.
polish_items.fix: Use the name of the actual currently-running tool call instead: const runningTool = props.toolCalls.find(c => c.status === 'running')?.name ?? 'read'.

polish_items.issue: The button uses an inline SVG with a manual aria-labelledby pointing to a <title> element, but the title element uses id='scroll-icon-title' which will duplicate if multiple scroll buttons exist (they shouldn't, but the pattern is fragile). The lucide-solid ChevronDown is already available and used throughout — use it instead.
polish_items.fix: Replace the SVG with <ArrowDown class='w-5 h-5' /> from lucide-solid and a simple aria-label='Scroll to bottom' on the button, eliminating the SVG verbosity.

polish_items.issue: File watcher and clipboard watcher are both started in createEffect + on() which fire on every change to their dependencies, but stopFileWatcher / clipboardWatcher.stop() in the effect body only runs if the outer condition flips. If the directory changes while the watcher is running, startFileWatcher is called again without stopping the previous watcher first.
polish_items.fix: Add explicit stop calls at the start of each createEffect: void stopFileWatcher(); ... then conditionally restart. Or return an onCleanup from within the createEffect body.

summary: AVA's chat UI is technically sophisticated — the interleaved thinking+tools rendering, three-tier mid-stream messaging, streaming-safe markdown throttling, and context-aware tool grouping are genuine differentiators that no single competitor fully matches. The SolidJS reactivity model is used correctly in most places, with accessor props and fine-grained memos rather than coarse store subscriptions. The design token system is thorough and the component library covers all necessary states (streaming, error, cancelled, thinking, tool-running, tool-error, diff, command output). The primary technical debt clusters around three areas: (1) MarkdownContent's innerHTML mutation bypasses reactivity and has a subtle unmount-race bug; (2) there is no true virtual list, only windowed-load, which will degrade at scale; (3) several small components (CommandOutputRow timer, ThinkingRow duration, suggestion card hover) have correctness or polish issues that are individually minor but accumulate into a rougher feel than the overall architecture deserves. The visual polish level is high — animation tokens, motion reductions, and themed variants are all implemented — but the streaming-to-settled transition jank and lack of animated collapse on tool cards are the most user-visible gaps. Closing the innerHTML/reactivity gap and adding animated collapse would have the highest perceived quality impact.
